item.title: Topological origin of Floquet thermalization in periodically driven many-body systems
item.description:
Author(s): Hao-Yue Qi, Yue Wu, and Wei Zheng

Floquet engineering is a powerful manipulation method in modern quantum technology. However, unwanted heating remains a major challenge of Floquet engineering, motivating extensive studies of Floquet thermalization. In this work, we investigate the thermalization of periodically driven many-body sys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094307] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao-Yue Qi, Yue Wu, and Wei Zheng

Floquet engineering is a powerful manipulation method in modern quantum technology. However, unwanted heating remains a major challenge of Floquet engineering, motivating extensive studies of Floquet thermalization. In this work, we investigate the thermalization of periodically driven many-body sys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094307] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological origin of Floquet thermalization in periodically driven many-body systems
item.dc:creator: Hao-Yue Qi, Yue Wu, and Wei Zheng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Dynamics, dynamical systems, lattice effects
item.prism:section: Dynamics, dynamical systems, lattice effects

item.title: Magnetic order in the van der Waals magnet ${\mathrm{VCl}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Zeyu Kao, Yiqing Hao, Yimeng Gu, Lixing Chen, Enkang Zhang, Hao Zhang, Fabio Orlandi, Pascal Manuel, Junfeng Wang, Chao Dong, Chuanying Xi, Zefeng Chen, Changsong Xu, and Jun Zhao

Here, the authors report the synthesis of high-quality VCl3​ single crystals, a van der Waals honeycomb magnet exhibiting a structural transition at 103.7 K. Using neutron diffraction, they resolve a prominent zigzag magnetic order below 21.8 K, characterized by a specific 𝐤=(0, 0.5, 1) propagation vector. This zigzag order phase and an observed magnetization plateau establish VCl3​ as a premier platform for investigating anisotropic magnetism and field-induced phase transitions in two-dimensional honeycomb lattices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094428] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zeyu Kao, Yiqing Hao, Yimeng Gu, Lixing Chen, Enkang Zhang, Hao Zhang, Fabio Orlandi, Pascal Manuel, Junfeng Wang, Chao Dong, Chuanying Xi, Zefeng Chen, Changsong Xu, and Jun Zhao

Here, the authors report the synthesis of high-quality VCl3​ single crystals, a van der Waals honeycomb magnet exhibiting a structural transition at 103.7 K. Using neutron diffraction, they resolve a prominent zigzag magnetic order below 21.8 K, characterized by a specific 𝐤=(0, 0.5, 1) propagation vector. This zigzag order phase and an observed magnetization plateau establish VCl3​ as a premier platform for investigating anisotropic magnetism and field-induced phase transitions in two-dimensional honeycomb lattices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094428] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic order in the van der Waals magnet ${\mathrm{VCl}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: Zeyu Kao, Yiqing Hao, Yimeng Gu, Lixing Chen, Enkang Zhang, Hao Zhang, Fabio Orlandi, Pascal Manuel, Junfeng Wang, Chao Dong, Chuanying Xi, Zefeng Chen, Changsong Xu, and Jun Zhao

item.title: Electron spin resonance signatures of Dirac spinons in the Gd-substituted kagome antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Gd}}_{0.015}{\mathrm{Y}}_{0.985}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{3}{(\mathrm{OD})}_{6.5}{\mathrm{Br}}_{2.5}$
item.description:
Author(s): T. Y. Kim, C. Koo, K. Yu. Povarov, U. Jena, P. Khunita, S. A. Zvyagin, J. Wosnitza, and K.-Y. Choi

External stimuli offer a route to realize exotic states with nontrivial excitations in highly frustrated kagome antiferromagnets. Here, 1.5% ${\mathrm{Gd}}^{3+}$ ions are substituted for ${\mathrm{Y}}^{3+}$ ions in ${\mathrm{YCu}}_{3}$(OD)${}_{6.5}{\mathrm{Br}}_{2.5}$ (abbreviated as ${\mathrm{YCu}}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094429] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. Y. Kim, C. Koo, K. Yu. Povarov, U. Jena, P. Khunita, S. A. Zvyagin, J. Wosnitza, and K.-Y. Choi

External stimuli offer a route to realize exotic states with nontrivial excitations in highly frustrated kagome antiferromagnets. Here, 1.5% Gd3+ ions are substituted for Y3+ ions in YCu3(OD)6.5Br2.5 (abbreviated as YCu3), yielding Gd0.015Y0.985Cu3(OD)6.5Br2.5, where the Gd3+ ions act as a local pro…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094429] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Electron spin resonance signatures of Dirac spinons in the Gd-substituted kagome antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Gd}}_{0.015}{\mathrm{Y}}_{0.985}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{3}{(\mathrm{OD})}_{6.5}{\mathrm{Br}}_{2.5}$
item.dc:creator: T. Y. Kim, C. Koo, K. Yu. Povarov, U. Jena, P. Khunita, S. A. Zvyagin, J. Wosnitza, and K.-Y. Choi

item.title: Magnetic field and pressure tuning of the heavy fermion antiferromagnet CePdIn
item.description:
Author(s): Bin Shen, Feng Du, Rui Li, Hang Su, Yasuyuki Shimura, Takahiro Onimaru, Kazunori Umeo, Xin Lu, Toshiro Takabatake, Michael Smidman, and Huiqiu Yuan

Frustrated Kondo lattices are ideal platforms for studying how both the Kondo effect and quantum fluctuations compete with the magnetic exchange interactions that drive magnetic ordering. Here, we investigate the effect of tuning the heavy-fermion compound CePdIn, which crystallizes in the geometric…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115130] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bin Shen, Feng Du, Rui Li, Hang Su, Yasuyuki Shimura, Takahiro Onimaru, Kazunori Umeo, Xin Lu, Toshiro Takabatake, Michael Smidman, and Huiqiu Yuan

Frustrated Kondo lattices are ideal platforms for studying how both the Kondo effect and quantum fluctuations compete with the magnetic exchange interactions that drive magnetic ordering. Here, we investigate the effect of tuning the heavy-fermion compound CePdIn, which crystallizes in the geometric…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115130] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Feynman paradox in a spherical axion insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Anastasiia Chyzhykova, Jeroen van den Brink, and Flavio S. Nogueira

The Feynman paradox highlights that static electromagnetic fields can store angular momentum. Here, the authors show that axion electrodynamics in a spherical topological insulator provides a topological realization of this effect. Varying the distance of a nearby charged probe induces Hall currents on the surface that convert electromagnetic angular momentum into mechanical rotation of the sphere, with a frequency derived from the topological response.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115414] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anastasiia Chyzhykova, Jeroen van den Brink, and Flavio S. Nogueira

The Feynman paradox highlights that static electromagnetic fields can store angular momentum. Here, the authors show that axion electrodynamics in a spherical topological insulator provides a topological realization of this effect. Varying the distance of a nearby charged probe induces Hall currents on the surface that convert electromagnetic angular momentum into mechanical rotation of the sphere, with a frequency derived from the topological response.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115414] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Feynman paradox in a spherical axion insulator
item.dc:creator: Anastasiia Chyzhykova, Jeroen van den Brink, and Flavio S. Nogueira

item.title: Ultrafast nonlinear optical response of twisted bilayer graphene quantum dots
item.description:
Author(s): Krishna Rana Magar and Vadym Apalkov

We study theoretically the nonlinear optical response of twisted AA- and AB-stacked bilayer graphene quantum dots placed in the field of an ultrashort optical pulse. We consider the cases of both linearly and circularly polarized pulses, and we characterize the nonlinear optical response of the syst…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125420] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Krishna Rana Magar and Vadym Apalkov

We study theoretically the nonlinear optical response of twisted AA- and AB-stacked bilayer graphene quantum dots placed in the field of an ultrashort optical pulse. We consider the cases of both linearly and circularly polarized pulses, and we characterize the nonlinear optical response of the syst…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125420] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Ultrafast nonlinear optical response of twisted bilayer graphene quantum dots

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Floquet engineering magnetism and superconductivity in the square-lattice Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Jan-Niklas Herre, Takuya Okugawa, Ammon Fischer, Christoph Karrasch, and Dante M. Kennes

The authors study here the interplay of magnetic order and superconductivity in the periodically driven square-lattice Hubbard model. In their Floquet random-phase approximation, circularly polarized light reshapes magnetic fluctuations inducing an antiferromagnetic-to-ferromagnetic transition close to a Floquet-Lifshitz transition. Near the antiferromagnetic phase, the light-dressed magnetic fluctuations stabilize topological d+id superconductivity at low driving frequencies, while high-frequency driving tunes pairing towards spin-triplet p wave. The work establishes a route to Floquet engineered topological superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121103] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jan-Niklas Herre, Takuya Okugawa, Ammon Fischer, Christoph Karrasch, and Dante M. Kennes

The authors study here the interplay of magnetic order and superconductivity in the periodically driven square-lattice Hubbard model. In their Floquet random-phase approximation, circularly polarized light reshapes magnetic fluctuations inducing an antiferromagnetic-to-ferromagnetic transition close to a Floquet-Lifshitz transition. Near the antiferromagnetic phase, the light-dressed magnetic fluctuations stabilize topological d+id superconductivity at low driving frequencies, while high-frequency driving tunes pairing towards spin-triplet p wave. The work establishes a route to Floquet engineered topological superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121103] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Floquet engineering magnetism and superconductivity in the square-lattice Hubbard model
item.dc:creator: Jan-Niklas Herre, Takuya Okugawa, Ammon Fischer, Christoph Karrasch, and Dante M. Kennes

item.title: Stress-induced martensitic transformation in epitaxial Ni-Mn-Ga thin films and its correlation to optical and magneto-optical properties
item.description:
Author(s): M. Makeš, J. Zázvorka, M. Hubert, P. Veřtát, M. Rameš, J. Zemen, O. Heczko, and M. Veis

The optical and magneto-optical properties of thin epitaxial Ni-Mn-Ga films, with thicknesses ranging from 8 to 160 nm, were investigated across the spectral range of 0.7–6.4 eV. The films were deposited by dc magnetron sputtering on MgO (100) substrates with a stress-mediating Cr buffer layer. Stru…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104420] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:

The films were deposited by dc magnetron sputtering on MgO (100) substrates with a stress-mediating Cr buffer layer. Stru…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104420] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

item.title: Tangle of spin double helices in the honeycomb Kitaev-$\mathrm{Γ}$ model
item.description:
Author(s): Jheng-Wei Li, Nihal Rao, Jan von Delft, Lode Pollet, and Ke Liu

We investigate the ground-state nature of the honeycomb Kitaev-$\mathrm{Γ}$ model in the material-relevant parameter regime through a combination of analytics and classical and quantum simulations. We find the classical model is imprinted with a tangle of highly structured spin double helices. This …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104423] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jheng-Wei Li, Nihal Rao, Jan von Delft, Lode Pollet, and Ke Liu

We investigate the ground-state nature of the honeycomb Kitaev-Γ model in the material-relevant parameter regime through a combination of analytics and classical and quantum simulations. We find the classical model is imprinted with a tangle of highly structured spin double helices. This helix tangl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104423] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

item.title: Universal inverted hysteresis induced by interfacial noncollinearity: From metallic exchange-spring bilayers to ferromagnet/topological insulator heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Guodong Wei, Hanqing Gong, Ligang Bai, Hangtian Wang, Zhiqin Zhou, Weiran Xie, Hanyu Huang, Jin Zou, Weisheng Zhao, Yanxue Chen, and Tianxiao Nie

Inverted hysteresis loops (IHLs) exhibiting negative coercivity and remanence represent a counterintuitive phenomenon in magnetic systems, with origins remaining elusive. Here, we address this long-standing paradox through combined experimental and theoretical investigations of two prototypical hete…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104424] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

Inverted hysteresis loops (IHLs) exhibiting negative coercivity and remanence represent a counterintuitive phenomenon in magnetic systems, with origins remaining elusive. Here, we address this long-standing paradox through combined experimental and theoretical investigations of two prototypical hete…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104424] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

item.title: Observation of magnetic polarons in ${\mathrm{EuB}}_{6}$ by small-angle neutron scattering
item.description:
Author(s): Gabrielle Beaudin, Alexandre Désilets-Benoit, Andrea Daniele Bianchi, Robert Arnold, Stavros Samothrakitis, Nikola Anna Galvan Leos, Jorge L. Gavilano, Michel Kenzelmann, Kilian D. Stenning, Mark Laver, Simon Gerber, Michael Nicklas, Robert Cubitt, and Charles Dewhurst

We conducted small-angle neutron scattering (SANS) experiments on the ferromagnetic semimetal ${\mathrm{EuB}}_{6}$, where we observed first-hand evidence for the presence of magnetic polarons. We carried out SANS experiments over a large range of scattering vectors $|\stackrel{⃗}{\mathbf{q}}|$, rang…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115127] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabrielle Beaudin, Alexandre Désilets-Benoit, Andrea Daniele Bianchi, Robert Arnold, Stavros Samothrakitis, Nikola Anna Galvan Leos, Jorge L. Gavilano, Michel Kenzelmann, Kilian D. Stenning, Mark Laver, Simon Gerber, Michael Nicklas, Robert Cubitt, and Charles Dewhurst

We conducted small-angle neutron scattering (SANS) experiments on the ferromagnetic semimetal EuB6, where we observed first-hand evidence for the presence of magnetic polarons. We carried out SANS experiments over a large range of scattering vectors |q⃗|, ranging from 0.006 to 0.140 Å−1 , and temper…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115127] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Observation of magnetic polarons in ${\mathrm{EuB}}_{6}$ by small-angle neutron scattering

item.title: Designing second-order topological insulators in heterobilayers via interlayer coupling and out-of-plane magnetic fields
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoliang Xiao, Weixiang Kong, Jiali Yang, Jin-Zhu Zhao, and Yuanjun Jin

The Zeeman effect, induced by the magnetic field, which can break the Dirac edge states of first-order topological insulators to generate a nontrivial gap, is a physical mechanism for the realization of two-dimensional second-order topological insulators (SOTIs). However, such magnetic-field-driven …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115128] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoliang Xiao, Weixiang Kong, Jiali Yang, Jin-Zhu Zhao, and Yuanjun Jin

The Zeeman effect, induced by the magnetic field, which can break the Dirac edge states of first-order topological insulators to generate a nontrivial gap, is a physical mechanism for the realization of two-dimensional second-order topological insulators (SOTIs). However, such magnetic-field-driven …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115128] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

item.title: Evanescent mode assisted Klein tunneling in dual-gated bilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Yupeng Huang and W. Zeng

We theoretically investigate the electron tunneling in dual-gated bilayer graphene-based $n/p$ junctions. It is shown that a band gap is introduced by tuning the gate voltage, which modifies the pseudospin polarization and breaks anti-Klein tunneling at normal incidence. Specifically, when the pseud…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115412] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

Author(s): Yupeng Huang and W. Zeng

We theoretically investigate the electron tunneling in dual-gated bilayer graphene-based n/p junctions. It is shown that a band gap is introduced by tuning the gate voltage, which modifies the pseudospin polarization and breaks anti-Klein tunneling at normal incidence. Specifically, when the pseudos…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115412] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

item.title: Non-onsite symmetry breaking: Topological phase coexistence and criticality
item.description:
Author(s): Zhehao Zhang, Yabo Li, and Tsung-Cheng Lu

We explore the states of matter arising from the spontaneous symmetry breaking (SSB) of ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ non-onsite symmetries. In one spatial dimension, we construct a frustration-free lattice model exhibiting SSB of a non-onsite symmetry, which features the coexistence of two ground states with …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125123] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhehao Zhang, Yabo Li, and Tsung-Cheng Lu

We explore the states of matter arising from the spontaneous symmetry breaking (SSB) of Z2 non-onsite symmetries. In one spatial dimension, we construct a frustration-free lattice model exhibiting SSB of a non-onsite symmetry, which features the coexistence of two ground states with distinct symmetr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125123] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026

item.title: Quantum phase diagram of the extended spin-$\frac{3}{2}$ Kitaev-Heisenberg model: A DMRG study
item.description:
Author(s): Gui-Xin Liu, Ting-Long Wang, and Yi-Fan Jiang

Recently, there has been considerable excitement surrounding the promising realization of high-spin Kitaev material, such as the quasi-2D compound ${\mathrm{CrI}}_{3}$ and ${\mathrm{CrGeTe}}_{3}$. However, the stability of quantum spin liquids (QSL) against single-ion anisotropy (SIA) in these mater…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125125] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gui-Xin Liu, Ting-Long Wang, and Yi-Fan Jiang

Recently, there has been considerable excitement surrounding the promising realization of high-spin Kitaev material, such as the quasi-2D compound CrI3 and CrGeTe3. However, the stability of quantum spin liquids (QSL) against single-ion anisotropy (SIA) in these materials and the global quantum phas…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125125] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum phase diagram of the extended spin-$\frac{3}{2}$ Kitaev-Heisenberg model: A DMRG study

item.title: Experimental confirmation of the magnetic ordering transition induced by an electronic structure change in the metallic triangular antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Co}}_{1/3}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Han-Jin Noh, En-Jin Cho, Byeong-Gyu Park, Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin, Cristian D. Batista, Pyeongjae Park, Woonghee Cho, and Je-Geun Park

We report angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) studies combined with $\mathrm{DFT}+\mathrm{DMFT}$ calculations to confirm that the magnetic ordering vector transition from $\mathrm{Q}=(1/2,0,0)$ to $\mathrm{Q}=(1/3,0,0)$ in the metallic triangular antiferromagnets ${\mathrm{Co}}_{1/3±ε}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125126] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Han-Jin Noh, En-Jin Cho, Byeong-Gyu Park, Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin, Cristian D. Batista, Pyeongjae Park, Woonghee Cho, and Je-Geun Park

We report angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) studies combined with DFT+DMFT calculations to confirm that the magnetic ordering vector transition from Q=(1/2,0,0) to Q=(1/3,0,0) in the metallic triangular antiferromagnets Co1/3±εTaS2 (ε≈0.007) is induced by the electronic structure cha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125126] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Experimental confirmation of the magnetic ordering transition induced by an electronic structure change in the metallic triangular antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{Co}}_{1/3}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Han-Jin Noh, En-Jin Cho, Byeong-Gyu Park, Hyowon Park, Ivar Martin, Cristian D. Batista, Pyeongjae Park, Woonghee Cho, and Je-Geun Park

item.title: Switchable chiral pair density wave in pure ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Wei Song, Xiao-Yu Yan, Xin Yu, Desheng Wu, Deng Hu, Hailang Qin, Guowei Liu, Hanbin Deng, Chao Yan, Muwei Gao, Zhiwei Wang, Rui Wu, and Jia-Xin Yin

We investigate electron pairing in a super clean kagome superconductor ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ with a residual resistivity ratio (RRR) of 290. Using the dilution-refrigerator-based scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) at the Synergetic Extreme Condition User Facility (SECUF), we find th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125127] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei Song, Xiao-Yu Yan, Xin Yu, Desheng Wu, Deng Hu, Hailang Qin, Guowei Liu, Hanbin Deng, Chao Yan, Muwei Gao, Zhiwei Wang, Rui Wu, and Jia-Xin Yin

We investigate electron pairing in a super clean kagome superconductor CsV3Sb5 with a residual resistivity ratio (RRR) of 290. Using the dilution-refrigerator-based scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) at the Synergetic Extreme Condition User Facility (SECUF), we find that the pairing gap exhibits ch…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125127] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Switchable chiral pair density wave in pure ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Wei Song, Xiao-Yu Yan, Xin Yu, Desheng Wu, Deng Hu, Hailang Qin, Guowei Liu, Hanbin Deng, Chao Yan, Muwei Gao, Zhiwei Wang, Rui Wu, and Jia-Xin Yin

item.title: Strongly nonlinear Bernstein modes in graphene reveal plasmon-enhanced near-field magnetoabsorption
item.description:
Author(s): I. Yahniuk, I. A. Dmitriev, A. L. Shilov, E. Mönch, M. Marocko, J. Eroms, D. Weiss, P. Sadovyi, B. Sadovyi, I. Grzegory, W. Knap, J. Gumenjuk-Sichevska, J. Wunderlich, D. A. Bandurin, and S. D. Ganichev

Bernstein modes—hybrid magnetoplasmon excitations arising from the coupling between cyclotron motion and collective oscillations in two-dimensional electron systems—offer direct access to nonlocal electrodynamics. These modes can exhibit rich nonlinear behavior akin to strong-coupling phenomena in c…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125418] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): I. Yahniuk, I. A. Dmitriev, A. L. Shilov, E. Mönch, M. Marocko, J. Eroms, D. Weiss, P. Sadovyi, B. Sadovyi, I. Grzegory, W. Knap, J. Gumenjuk-Sichevska, J. Wunderlich, D. A. Bandurin, and S. D. Ganichev

Bernstein modes—hybrid magnetoplasmon excitations arising from the coupling between cyclotron motion and collective oscillations in two-dimensional electron systems—offer direct access to nonlocal electrodynamics. These modes can exhibit rich nonlinear behavior akin to strong-coupling phenomena in c…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125418] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Strongly nonlinear Bernstein modes in graphene reveal plasmon-enhanced near-field magnetoabsorption
item.dc:creator: I. Yahniuk, I. A. Dmitriev, A. L. Shilov, E. Mönch, M. Marocko, J. Eroms, D. Weiss, P. Sadovyi, B. Sadovyi, I. Grzegory, W. Knap, J. Gumenjuk-Sichevska, J. Wunderlich, D. A. Bandurin, and S. D. Ganichev

item.title: Analytic quantifying chirality of Néel-type spin twists using interactions between stray fields and magnetic tips
item.description:
Author(s): Lingyao Kong, Chunhui Zhang, Weiwei Wang, Yaodong Wu, and Jin Tang

Magnetic chirality describes the fixed correspondence between polarization and rotation of noncollinear spin twists. It remains a fundamental challenge in determining the chirality of Néel-twisted nanometric spin textures. This work presents a theoretical framework for determining the chirality of N…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094419] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lingyao Kong, Chunhui Zhang, Weiwei Wang, Yaodong Wu, and Jin Tang

Magnetic chirality describes the fixed correspondence between polarization and rotation of noncollinear spin twists. It remains a fundamental challenge in determining the chirality of Néel-twisted nanometric spin textures. This work presents a theoretical framework for determining the chirality of N…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094419] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026

item.title: Emergence of multiple topological spin textures in an all-magnetic van der Waals heterostructure
item.description:
Author(s): Moritz A. Goerzen, Tim Drevelow, Hendrik Schrautzer, Soumyajyoti Haldar, Stefan Heinze, and Dongzhe Li

Magnetic solitons such as skyrmions and bimerons show great promise for both fundamental research and spintronic applications. Stabilizing and controlling topological spin textures in atomically thin van der Waals (vdW) materials has gained tremendous attention due to high tunability, enhanced funct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094420] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Moritz A. Goerzen, Tim Drevelow, Hendrik Schrautzer, Soumyajyoti Haldar, Stefan Heinze, and Dongzhe Li

Magnetic solitons such as skyrmions and bimerons show great promise for both fundamental research and spintronic applications. Stabilizing and controlling topological spin textures in atomically thin van der Waals (vdW) materials has gained tremendous attention due to high tunability, enhanced funct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094420] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026

item.title: Tunable multimagnon Floquet topological edge states
item.description:
Author(s): I. Martinez-Berumen, T. Pereg-Barnea, and W. A. Coish

We show that periodically time-modulating the Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) in a two-dimensional magnon insulator may induce a topological phase transition that results in the presence of robust edge modes. To this end, we study a square lattice of spins interacting via an XXZ Heisenberg m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104419] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): I. Martinez-Berumen, T. Pereg-Barnea, and W. A. Coish

We show that periodically time-modulating the Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) in a two-dimensional magnon insulator may induce a topological phase transition that results in the presence of robust edge modes. To this end, we study a square lattice of spins interacting via an XXZ Heisenberg m…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104419] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunable multimagnon Floquet topological edge states

item.title: Constructing a variational ground state of matter fermions coupled to a vison pair in Kitaev's honeycomb model
item.description:
Author(s): Theo Grace and Alessandro Principi

We develop a method to construct simple and explicit variational approximations for the ground state of Kitaev's honeycomb model with a nontrivial ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ flux configuration consisting of a pair of visons on neighboring plaquettes. The method consists of retaining only the largest singula…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115122] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Theo Grace and Alessandro Principi

We develop a method to construct simple and explicit variational approximations for the ground state of Kitaev's honeycomb model with a nontrivial Z2 flux configuration consisting of a pair of visons on neighboring plaquettes. The method consists of retaining only the largest singular values of the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115122] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026

item.title: Quantum path-integral method for the fictitious-particle Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Zhijie Fan, Tianning Xiao, and Youjin Deng

We formulate a path-integral Monte Carlo algorithm for simulating lattice systems consisting of fictitious particles governed by generalized exchange statistics. This method, initially proposed for continuum systems, introduces a continuous parameter $ξ$ in the partition function that interpolates b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115123] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026

We formulate a path-integral Monte Carlo algorithm for simulating lattice systems consisting of fictitious particles governed by generalized exchange statistics. This method, initially proposed for continuum systems, introduces a continuous parameter ξ in the partition function that interpolates bet…

item.title: One-dimensional moiré engineering in zigzag graphene nanoribbons on $h\text{−}\mathrm{BN}$
item.description:
Author(s): Ryosuke Okumura, Naoto Nakatsuji, Takuto Kawakami, and Mikito Koshino

We study the structural relaxation and electronic properties of a one-dimensional (1D) moiré system composed of a zigzag graphene nanoribbon (GNR) placed on a hexagonal boron nitride $(h\text{−}\mathrm{BN})$ substrate. Using an effective grid model derived from continuum elasticity theory, we calcul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115411] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ryosuke Okumura, Naoto Nakatsuji, Takuto Kawakami, and Mikito Koshino

We study the structural relaxation and electronic properties of a one-dimensional (1D) moiré system composed of a zigzag graphene nanoribbon (GNR) placed on a hexagonal boron nitride (h−BN) substrate. Using an effective grid model derived from continuum elasticity theory, we calculate the relaxed at…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115411] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: One-dimensional moiré engineering in zigzag graphene nanoribbons on $h\text{−}\mathrm{BN}$

item.title: Breakdown of chiral anomaly and emergent phases in Weyl semimetals under orbital magnetic fields
item.description:
Author(s): Faruk Abdulla, Anna Keselman, and Daniel Podolsky

An external orbital magnetic field applied perpendicular to the separation vector of a pair of Weyl points can couple them and induce a gap in the electronic spectrum. In this work, we investigate the gap-opening behavior in the presence of a lattice, revealing rich phenomenology absent in the conti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125121] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Faruk Abdulla, Anna Keselman, and Daniel Podolsky

An external orbital magnetic field applied perpendicular to the separation vector of a pair of Weyl points can couple them and induce a gap in the electronic spectrum. In this work, we investigate the gap-opening behavior in the presence of a lattice, revealing rich phenomenology absent in the conti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125121] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026

item.title: Impact of a photoemission resonance on the measured transition timescale in solid state photoemission
item.description:
Author(s): Fei Guo, Dmitry Usanov, Eduardo B. Guedes, Arnaud Magrez, Michele Puppin, and J. Hugo Dil

Fundamental quantum transition timescales are accessible through the spin polarization of photoelectrons coming from initially spin-degenerate states for solid-state materials. In this work we investigate the modification of this timescale in the vicinity of a resonance in photoemission from a solid…

item.title: Effect of uniaxial stress on helimagnetic phases in the square-lattice itinerant magnet ${\mathrm{EuAl}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Masaki Gen, Takuya Nomoto, Hiraku Saito, Taro Nakajima, Yusuke Tokunaga, Rina Takagi, Shinichiro Seki, and Taka-hisa Arima

We investigate uniaxial-stress effects on the magnetic phase diagram of the square-lattice itinerant magnet ${\mathrm{EuAl}}_{4}$, where strong coupling among spin, lattice, and charge produces a variety of helimagnetic phases, including rhombic and square skyrmion lattices. Combining resistivity an…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100406] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Masaki Gen, Takuya Nomoto, Hiraku Saito, Taro Nakajima, Yusuke Tokunaga, Rina Takagi, Shinichiro Seki, and Taka-hisa Arima

We investigate uniaxial-stress effects on the magnetic phase diagram of the square-lattice itinerant magnet EuAl4, where strong coupling among spin, lattice, and charge produces a variety of helimagnetic phases, including rhombic and square skyrmion lattices. Combining resistivity and magnetization …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100406] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Effect of uniaxial stress on helimagnetic phases in the square-lattice itinerant magnet ${\mathrm{EuAl}}_{4}$

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Intimate relationship between spin configuration in the triplet pair and superconductivity in ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Hiroki Matsumura, Yuki Takahashi, Riku Matsubayashi, Katsuki Kinjo, Shunsaku Kitagawa, Kenji Ishida, Yo Tokunaga, Hironori Sakai, Shinsaku Kambe, Motoi Kimata, Ai Nakamura, Yusei Shimizu, Yoshiya Homma, Dexin Li, Fuminori Honda, Atsushi Miyake, Dai Aoki, Tetsuya Furukawa, and Takahiro Sasaki

Spin-triplet superconductivity exhibits rich spin degrees of freedom, yet its field response remains unresolved. Here, the authors report Knight shift and ac susceptibility measurements on UTe2, revealing a rapid recovery of spin susceptibility near 5 T (H || c), well below Hc2. Concomitantly, Hc2 increases when triplet spins align with the field. These results demonstrate a direct link between spin configuration and superconducting stability, indicating anisotropic pinning intrinsic to triplet pairing and drawing parallels with superfluid 3He.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094506] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hiroki Matsumura, Yuki Takahashi, Riku Matsubayashi, Katsuki Kinjo, Shunsaku Kitagawa, Kenji Ishida, Yo Tokunaga, Hironori Sakai, Shinsaku Kambe, Motoi Kimata, Ai Nakamura, Yusei Shimizu, Yoshiya Homma, Dexin Li, Fuminori Honda, Atsushi Miyake, Dai Aoki, Tetsuya Furukawa, and Takahiro Sasaki

Spin-triplet superconductivity exhibits rich spin degrees of freedom, yet its field response remains unresolved. Here, the authors report Knight shift and ac susceptibility measurements on UTe2, revealing a rapid recovery of spin susceptibility near 5 T (H || c), well below Hc2. Concomitantly, Hc2 increases when triplet spins align with the field. These results demonstrate a direct link between spin configuration and superconducting stability, indicating anisotropic pinning intrinsic to triplet pairing and drawing parallels with superfluid 3He.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094506] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Intimate relationship between spin configuration in the triplet pair and superconductivity in ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Hiroki Matsumura, Yuki Takahashi, Riku Matsubayashi, Katsuki Kinjo, Shunsaku Kitagawa, Kenji Ishida, Yo Tokunaga, Hironori Sakai, Shinsaku Kambe, Motoi Kimata, Ai Nakamura, Yusei Shimizu, Yoshiya Homma, Dexin Li, Fuminori Honda, Atsushi Miyake, Dai Aoki, Tetsuya Furukawa, and Takahiro Sasaki

item.title: High-pressure and magnetism in the quasi-one-dimensional solid solution ${\mathrm{Ca}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{Na}}_{x}{\mathrm{Cr}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$: A multimodal neutron, muon, and x-ray study
item.description:
Author(s): E. Nocerino, H. Sakurai, O. K. Forslund, K. Papadopoulos, D. J. Mukkattukavil, D. Andreica, G. Simutis, R. Khasanov, N. Ishimatsu, N. Kawamura, C. L. Bull, N. P. Funnell, J. Sugiyama, I. Umegaki, Y. Sassa, and M. Månsson

We investigate the pressure-dependent magnetism of the quasi-one-dimensional solid solution ${\mathrm{Ca}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{Na}}_{x}{\mathrm{Cr}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$ using neutron diffraction, Cr K-edge x-ray absorption spectroscopy, and muon spin rotation/relaxation. Na substitution is known t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104414] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): E. Nocerino, H. Sakurai, O. K. Forslund, K. Papadopoulos, D. J. Mukkattukavil, D. Andreica, G. Simutis, R. Khasanov, N. Ishimatsu, N. Kawamura, C. L. Bull, N. P. Funnell, J. Sugiyama, I. Umegaki, Y. Sassa, and M. Månsson

We investigate the pressure-dependent magnetism of the quasi-one-dimensional solid solution Ca1−xNaxCr2O4 using neutron diffraction, Cr K-edge x-ray absorption spectroscopy, and muon spin rotation/relaxation. Na substitution is known to increase oxygen-ligand hole density (partial Cr3+ to Cr4+ chara…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104414] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: High-pressure and magnetism in the quasi-one-dimensional solid solution ${\mathrm{Ca}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{Na}}_{x}{\mathrm{Cr}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$: A multimodal neutron, muon, and x-ray study

item.title: Quantum geometry in low-energy linear and nonlinear optical responses of the magnetic Rashba semiconductor (Ge,Mn)Te
item.description:
Author(s): Tsubasa Takagi, Hikaru Watanabe, Ryutaro Yoshimi, Yuki Sato, Shingo Toyoda, Atsushi Tsukazaki, Kei S. Takahashi, Masashi Kawasaki, Yoshinori Tokura, and Naoki Ogawa

Quantum geometry appears as a key factor in understanding the optical properties of quantum materials, with the anticipation on diverging or quantized responses near the Dirac and Weyl points. Here, we investigate linear and nonlinear optical responses—optical conductivity and injection current—in a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104417] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026

Quantum geometry appears as a key factor in understanding the optical properties of quantum materials, with the anticipation on diverging or quantized responses near the Dirac and Weyl points. Here, we investigate linear and nonlinear optical responses—optical conductivity and injection current—in a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104417] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026

item.title: Chiral anomaly induced nonlinear transverse planar transport phenomena in three-dimensional spin-orbit coupled metals
item.description:
Author(s): Rishi G. Gopalakrishnan, Binayyak B. Roy, Gargee Sharma, and Sumanta Tewari

We investigate the nonlinear transverse planar transport phenomena (viz., nonlinear Hall, thermal Hall, and Nernst coefficients) induced by chiral anomaly in three-dimensional spin-orbit coupled metallic systems. Unlike Weyl semimetals, these systems do not possess multiple Weyl nodes located at iso…

Unlike Weyl semimetals, these systems do not possess multiple Weyl nodes located at iso…

item.title: Emergence of nematic loop-current bond order in kagome metals near van Hove singularities
item.description:
Author(s): Alex Friedlan and Hae-Young Kee

The recently discovered family of kagome metals has attracted significant interest as a result of reports of charge-bond order, orbital magnetism, and superconductivity. Some of these phases may exhibit time-reversal symmetry breaking. More recently, experiments have reported the emergence of nemati…

item.title: Magnetic control of Dirac points emerging in carbon nanoscrolls
item.description:

Dirac points close to the Fermi level can cause geometrical quantities, such as the Berry curvature and quantum metric, to diverge, thereby significantly influencing the transverse electronic responses of the system. Theoretical studies have demonstrated that Dirac points can emerge in the band stru…

Theoretical studies have demonstrated that Dirac points can emerge in the band stru…

The Wiedemann-Franz (WF) law, relating the electronic thermal conductivity (${κ}_{\mathrm{e}}$) to the electrical conductivity, is vital in numerous applications such as in the design of thermoelectric materials and in the experimental determination of the lattice thermal conductivity (${κ}_{\mathrm…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121102] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Luyao Zhong, Xin Jin, Mingquan He, Rui Wang, Xiaoyuan Zhou, Tianqi Deng, and Xiaolong Yang

The Wiedemann-Franz (WF) law, relating the electronic thermal conductivity (κe) to the electrical conductivity, is vital in numerous applications such as in the design of thermoelectric materials and in the experimental determination of the lattice thermal conductivity (κL). While the WF law is gene…

The introduction of the valley degree of freedom confers the typical valley-selective excitation characte…

item.title: Detecting quantum spin liquid on Kitaev model through a superconducting junction
item.description:
Author(s): A. R. Moura and L. V. Santos

The Kitaev model belongs to an unconventional class of two-dimensional spin systems characterized by anisotropic, bond-dependent interactions that give rise to quantum spin liquid (QSL) states. These exotic phases, marked by the absence of magnetic ordering even at zero temperature, support fraction…

These exotic phases, marked by the absence of magnetic ordering even at zero temperature, support fraction…

item.title: Strain-enhanced edge ferromagnetism and bipolar magnetic semiconducting behavior in Janus graphene nanoribbons
item.description:
Author(s): Ran Liu, Hongxing Liu, Junfeng Ren, and Tianxing Ma

Using first-principles density functional theory and determinant quantum Monte Carlo methods, we show that Janus graphene nanoribbons with topological defect arrays ($m=2$) exhibit robust intrinsic ferromagnetism across widths $W=2\text{–}6$, with bandgaps exceeding $200\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}\…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094414] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ran Liu, Hongxing Liu, Junfeng Ren, and Tianxing Ma

Using first-principles density functional theory and determinant quantum Monte Carlo methods, we show that Janus graphene nanoribbons with topological defect arrays (m=2) exhibit robust intrinsic ferromagnetism across widths W=2–6, with bandgaps exceeding 200meV and stable ferromagnetic ground state…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094414] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Strain-enhanced edge ferromagnetism and bipolar magnetic semiconducting behavior in Janus graphene nanoribbons
item.dc:creator: Ran Liu, Hongxing Liu, Junfeng Ren, and Tianxing Ma
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism
